授業目的: The purpose of this course is for students to improve their English reading comprehension skills by applying knowledge of existing and new vocabulary and comprehending main ideas and key points from reading passages. By focusing on the 17 SDG's, content featured throughout the course will help students develop global perspectives.
到達目標: 1. Reading Comprehension (To develop the ability to read English passages and correctly understand their content)2. Extensive Reading Ability (To develop the ability to read through large volumes of English text in a short time)3. Vocabulary (To memorize a large number of words and phrases required to correctly understand extensive English text)4. Critical Thinking (To develop global perspectives through critical thinking exercises with classmates)
授業方法: This course is a collaborative format meaning students will engage in pair/group discussions every class. Students will work individually first, but more time will be spent interacting with classmates.

学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: 各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 ※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）

成績評価Grading5: 備考　Note 1. Prerequisites: Taking the TOEIC test in May (1年生) or in December (2年生) required by KGU is the prerequisite for credit earning.2. Absences: 4 absences are considered excessive and result in automatic failure. 3. Tardiness: Coming into the classroom after the bell is considered tardiness. Arrival after 30 minutes is considered an absence. 2 tardy arrivals equal 1 absence. (Being late is not considered tardiness in case of documented delay of public transportation.)4. Make-up Assignments: Make-up assignments will not be given except in cases of documented medical necessity or family emergency. 5. Cheating: Cheating on any quizzes, tests, projects, or assignments results in zero point on the work.
備考Note0: 1. Classroom Participation: Leaving a classroom without asking for permission, doing something else that than class activities, or sleeping are considered an absence. 2. AI-based Tools: Aside from the usefulness of Artificial Intelligence (AI) content generators, using an AI-based tool (such as ChatGPT, DeepL Translate, and Google Translate) to complete your coursework without proper authorization is a form of academic dishonesty. It violates academic standards of Kansei Gakuin University. Thus, completing assignments and exams by using an AI-based tool is regarded cheating, which will result in a score of zero points on any coursework. 3. Portrait Rights and Copyrights: Taking a photo of the instructor, classmates, and any class material (like handouts, quizzes, and tests) and sharing it with somebody without permission is the violation of the portrait rights and copyrights.

備考　Note
1. Prerequisites: Taking the TOEIC test in May (1年生) or in December (2年生) required by KGU is the prerequisite for credit earning.
2. Absences: 4 absences are considered excessive and result in automatic failure. 
3. Tardiness: Coming into the classroom after the bell is considered tardiness. Arrival after 30 minutes is considered an absence. 2 tardy arrivals equal 1 absence. (Being late is not considered tardiness in case of documented delay of public transportation.)
4. Make-up Assignments: Make-up assignments will not be given except in cases of documented medical necessity or family emergency. 
5. Cheating: Cheating on any quizzes, tests, projects, or assignments results in zero point on the work.
